assertions.text: Another car pulled out in front of the narrator on the fast road.
The narrator was traveling at around 110 km/h before braking.
The emergency braking reduced speed from around 110 km/h down to walking pace within a few seconds.
There was no contact between the narrator's car and the other car (zero impacts).
After clearing the situation, the narrator accelerated and picked up speed again.
The collision did not occur, so no collision severity (no impact).

verdicts.evidence: The presence of 'another car pulled out' is not observable in these vehicle sensors; no external object detection channels are available, so this is [unverifiable] (no numeric citation possible).
GPS shows the vehicle speed at the start of the window was [113.0 km/h](T2) and the first gps sample is [113.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T3), which is within ~15% of the claimed [110 km/h](T3).

Detect_events found a sustained deceleration on accel_x from [t=0.22 to t=4.11 s] with mean magnitude [0.55 g](T4), and gps speed fell from [113.0 km/h to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0 s](T4), consistent with emergency braking.

GPS speed dropped from [113.0 km/h at t=0.0 s] to [10.0 km/h at t=8.0 s] with intermediary samples showing [55.0 km/h at t=4.0 s] and [38.0 km/h at t=5.0 s](T3); the compute_stats integrated accel axis shows an integrated delta-V of [−51.8 km/h on accel_x over the window](T2), indicating a reduction of about [51.8 km/h](T2) within a few seconds.

There is no crash-pulse or impact-level spike detected: detect_events did not report a CISS impact and accel peaks are below high-impact thresholds; gps_speed and accel traces show no abrupt post-impact anomalies — sensors indicate no recorded impact; additionally gps samples show continuous deceleration without post-impact discontinuity [gps first 113.0 to last 42.0 over window](T2).
After the low speed around [t=10.0–11.0 s where gps was 8.0–13.0 km/h], gps_speed rises to [42.0 km/h at t=15.0 s](T3), indicating the vehicle accelerated again after clearing the event.

Sensors show no CISS impact detection and no high-magnitude crash pulse; gps_speed shows no abrupt collision-induced discontinuity and overall data documents no impact — thus there was no collision recorded by sensors (gps first value [113.0 km/h], last [42.0 km/h] over window)(T2).

result.rationale: Telemetry records show a large, sustained deceleration from about [113.0 km/h](T2) with integrated delta-V on an accel axis of [−51.8 km/h](T2), multiple detected speed drops (e.g., [113.0→78.0 km/h between t=0–3 s](T4)), no CISS impact detected, and a later increase in gps speed to [42.0 km/h at t=15.0 s](T3), supporting most narrative points though external vehicle presence is not observable.

citations.claim: 113.0 km/h to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0 s
−51.8 km/h on accel_x over the window
gps first 113.0 to last 42.0 over window

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
